reasoning: NOP16 (also known as nucleolar protein 16) is primarily involved in ribosome biogenesis and nucleolar function. Its knockdown by CRISPRi generally leads to defects in ribosomal RNA processing and may trigger nucleolar stress, which can activate p53‑dependent pathways. GBP8 (guanylate‑binding protein 8) is an interferon‑stimulated GTPase that is transcriptionally induced by type II and type I interferons through the JAK‑STAT signaling cascade. There is no established mechanistic link between ribosome biogenesis factors such as NOP16 and the transcriptional regulation of interferon‑responsive genes like GBP8. Consequently, reducing NOP16 levels is unlikely to directly increase or decrease GBP8 expression; any effect would be indirect and weak, and most datasets would show no significant change.
